# @zakkster/lite-headless/dialog

createDialog(options) -> DialogHandle

## Options

- open: WriteSignal<boolean>     -- external controlled mode
- defaultOpen: boolean = false   -- initial state, uncontrolled
- onOpenChange(next, reason)     -- notifier; reason in: 'trigger'|'api'|'escape'|'outside'|'close'
- modal: boolean = true          -- role=dialog, aria-modal, scroll lock, focus trap
- closeOnEscape: boolean = true
- closeOnOutsideClick: boolean = true
- initialFocus: 'auto'|HTMLElement|string|false = 'auto'
- finalFocus: 'trigger'|HTMLElement|false = 'trigger'
- container: HTMLElement|null = document.body  -- portal target; null = render in place
- transition: boolean = false    -- status waits for transitionend when true
- labelledBy: string             -- aria-labelledby id
- describedBy: string            -- aria-describedby id

## Returned

{
    open: ReadSignal<boolean>,
    status: ReadSignal<'closed'|'opening'|'open'|'closing'|'closed'>,
    setOpen(value, reason),
    toggle(),
    attachTrigger(el): off,
    attachContent(el): off,
    attachClose(el): off,
    destroy(),
    destroyed: boolean
}

## Slot conventions (for <lite-dialog>)

- [data-trigger]: opens dialog on click
- [data-content]: the dialog panel (gets role/aria-modal/data-open/data-status)
- [data-close]: closes dialog on click (multiple allowed)

## <lite-dialog> attributes

- open               -- reflects state, reactive
- no-modal           -- modal:false
- no-escape          -- closeOnEscape:false
- no-outside         -- closeOnOutsideClick:false
- container          -- 'self'|'none' = in place; else selector
- transition         -- transition:true

## States exposed on content

- data-open: present when open (boolean)
- data-status: 'closed' | 'opening' | 'open' | 'closing'
- aria-hidden: 'true' when closed, removed when open

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
